definition: The sequencing of credit deterioration → equity decline → labor market weakening with 3-12 month leads is empirically robust across the 1990, 2000-01, and 2007-09 episodes. Grounded in both New Keynesian (external finance premium is forward-looking, labor is lagging) and Post-Keynesian (endogenous money creation → spending → income → profits → equity validation → labor adjustment) frameworks.
evidence: Theoretically grounded in both New Keynesian and Post-Keynesian frameworks
Credit-equity linkage analysis provides the structural mechanism for this previously empirical observation
open_questions: Has the sequencing timing changed with structural market evolution since 2010?
Does private credit opacity delay the credit signal in this sequence?
